R01.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R01 Deliver production-ready, modular, working code.md
R01.title: Deliver production-ready, modular, working code
R01.text: You will review, refactor as needed, and ensure all of the code is working, fully-functional, well-designed, modular, responsive, and ready for production.
R01.judgment: The umbrella quality mandate; every coding-standards rule ultimately refines this. Very broad as written.
R01.paraphrase: Review, refactor as needed, and ensure all code is working, fully-functional, well-designed, modular, responsive, and ready for production.

R02.title: Always follow best practices
R02.judgment: Annotator: 'what are best practices? looks like a parent rule.' Exactly — an unfalsifiable slogan whose meaning is entirely delegated to the concrete rules below.

R03.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R03 Think deeply and describe a detailed plan before building.md
R03.title: Think deeply and describe a detailed plan before building
R03.text: First think very deeply and thoroughly step-by-step - describe your careful and systematic plan for what to build in great detail.
R03.judgment: Plan-before-build duty. Annotator wants examples; vague but clearly prescriptive. Supports the Plan phase of the workflow.
R03.paraphrase: First think very deeply and thoroughly step-by-step - describe your careful and systematic plan for what to build in great detail.

R04.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R04 Core Workflow - Research to Validate.md
R04.title: Core Workflow - Research to Validate
R04.text: ### Core Workflow: Research → Plan → Implement → Validate
R04.judgment: The parent process. Hand tagged PREFERENCE, but it is the spine the four phase-rules hang off; the phases support it.
R04.paraphrase: Core Workflow: Research -> Plan -> Implement -> Validate.

R05.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R05 Start every feature by researching the codebase and planning.md
R05.title: Start every feature by researching the codebase and planning
R05.text: Start every feature with: "Let me research the codebase and create a plan before implementing."
R05.judgment: Entry trigger for the workflow; in chat becomes asking clarifying questions first.
R05.paraphrase: Start every feature with: 'Let me research the codebase and create a plan before implementing.'

R06.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R06 Research via systematic file exploration.md
R06.title: Research via systematic file exploration
R06.text: Research - Understand existing patterns and architecture through systematic file exploration
R06.paraphrase: Research - understand existing patterns and architecture through systematic file exploration.

R07.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R07 Plan - propose approach with reasoning, verify if uncertain.md
R07.title: Plan: propose approach with reasoning, verify if uncertain
R07.text: Plan - Propose approach with architectural reasoning and verify with me if uncertain
R07.judgment: Phase 2 rule; the 'verify if uncertain' clause links it to the collaboration/uncertainty rules.
R07.paraphrase: Plan - propose approach with architectural reasoning and verify with me if uncertain.

R08.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R08 Implement complete, production-ready solutions with tests.md
R08.title: Implement complete, production-ready solutions with tests
R08.text: Implement - Build complete, production-ready solutions with tests and error handling
R08.judgment: Phase 3 rule; a domain instance of R01's production-ready mandate.
R08.paraphrase: Implement - build complete, production-ready solutions with tests and error handling.

R09.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R09 Validate - ALWAYS run formatters, linters, and tests after implementing.md
R09.title: Validate: ALWAYS run formatters, linters, and tests after implementing
R09.text: Validate - ALWAYS run formatters, linters, and tests after implementation
R09.judgment: Phase 4 rule and a checkpoint on the implement phase — a mandated verify-moment with no new build obligation.
R09.paraphrase: Validate - ALWAYS run formatters, linters, and tests after implementation.

R10.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R10 3-Try Rule - stop and reflect after three failed attempts.md
R10.title: 3-Try Rule: stop and reflect after three failed attempts
R10.text: If we fail to achieve our goal within three tries (whether due to error messages or other issues), stop, step back, and reflect on precisely what information you need from me to best assist.
R10.judgment: A bounded STOP-and-ask rule — the rare concrete trigger (three tries) in an otherwise self-assessed file.
R10.paraphrase: If we fail to achieve our goal within three tries, stop, step back, and reflect on precisely what information you need from me.

R11.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R11 Gather information intelligently rather than guessing.md
R11.title: Gather information intelligently rather than guessing
R11.text: Rather than blindly reaching for solutions, intelligently gather information - ask for code output, files, context, or whatever else is necessary.
R11.judgment: The positive method behind R10 — how you spend the stop.
R11.paraphrase: Rather than blindly reaching for solutions, intelligently gather information - ask for code output, files, context, or whatever else is necessary.

R12.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R12 Attempt one logical fix per error before asking.md
R12.title: Attempt one logical fix per error before asking
R12.text: Attempt one logical fix per error before asking for guidance
R12.judgment: Concrete debugging cadence; bounds the trial-and-error loop.
R12.paraphrase: Attempt one logical fix per error before asking for guidance.

R13.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R13 Add and verify logs to validate assumptions.md
R13.title: Add and verify logs to validate assumptions
R13.text: Add and verify logs to validate assumptions
R13.judgment: Evidence rule; refined by the 5-7-sources procedure R14.
R13.paraphrase: Add and verify logs to validate assumptions.

R14.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R14 When stuck - weigh 5-7 sources, distill to 1-2, log before fixing.md
R14.title: When stuck: weigh 5-7 sources, distill to 1-2, log before fixing
R14.text: When stuck: "Reflect on 5-7 different possible sources of the problem, distill those down to 1-2 most likely sources, and then add logs to validate your assumptions before implementing the actual code fix"
R14.judgment: A concrete procedure operationalizing R13's 'validate assumptions'.
R14.paraphrase: When stuck: reflect on 5-7 possible sources, distill to 1-2 most likely, then add logs to validate before implementing the fix.

R15.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R15 If the first try fails, show and explain error outputs.md
R15.title: If the first try fails, show and explain error outputs
R15.text: If first try fails, show and explain error outputs
R15.judgment: Transparency duty inside the debugging loop.
R15.paraphrase: If first try fails, show and explain error outputs.

R16.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R16 Step-by-step planning - pseudocode, root-cause over quick fixes.md
R16.title: Step-by-step planning: pseudocode, root-cause over quick fixes
R16.text:
### Step-by-Step Planning
- Detailed pseudocode before implementation
- Root cause analysis over quick fixes
- Extensive planning before action with systematic methodology
R16.judgment: Hand-tagged PREFERENCE ('feels softer, super vague'). Restates the plan-first and root-cause themes; overlaps R03 and R36.
R16.paraphrase: Detailed pseudocode before implementation; root cause analysis over quick fixes; extensive planning with systematic methodology.

R17.title: Professional, direct, content-first tone
R17.text: - Professional yet direct tone, content-first with minimal fluff
R17.judgment: Style preference; parent for the directness rules.
R17.paraphrase: Professional yet direct tone, content-first with minimal fluff.

R18.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R18 Structured responses with clear formatting.md
R18.title: Structured responses with clear formatting
R18.judgment: Style preference; loosely related to the response-format templates.

R19.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R19 Treat mistakes as growth opportunities.md
R19.title: Treat mistakes as growth opportunities
R19.text: - Support learning process and treat mistakes as growth opportunities
R19.paraphrase: Support learning process and treat mistakes as growth opportunities.

R20.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R20 Point out mistakes directly rather than softening.md
R20.title: Point out mistakes directly rather than softening
R20.text: Directly point out mistakes in reasoning rather than softening corrections
R20.judgment: Concretizes the 'direct tone' preference into an actionable duty.
R20.paraphrase: Directly point out mistakes in reasoning rather than softening corrections.

R21.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R21 No opt-in follow-up suggestions unless relevant.md
R21.title: No opt-in follow-up suggestions unless relevant
R21.text: Don't end with opt-in follow-up suggestions unless specifically relevant
R21.judgment: Clear prohibition; conflicts with the end-template R23, which mandates a 'Next Task' suggestions block.
R21.paraphrase: Don't end with opt-in follow-up suggestions unless specifically relevant.

R22.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R22 Start responses with the Language>Specialist template.md
R22.title: Start responses with the Language>Specialist template
R22.text: Start responses with: Language > Specialist / Includes / Requirements / ## Plan ... (unless answering a quick question).
R22.judgment: A concrete output-format mandate (annotator: 'this all feels like a rule!'). Grounded by the template context.
R22.paraphrase: Start responses with: Language > Specialist / Includes / Requirements / ## Plan ... (unless answering a quick question).

R23.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R23 End responses with the History-Source Tree-Next Task template.md
R23.title: End responses with the History/Source Tree/Next Task template
R23.text: End responses with: History / Source Tree (with status emojis) / Next Task.
R23.judgment: Output-format mandate hand-tagged PREFERENCE. Its 'Next Task suggestions' clause directly conflicts with R21.
R23.paraphrase: End responses with: History / Source Tree (with status emojis) / Next Task.

R24.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R24 Code starts with a path-filename comment.md
R24.title: Code starts with a path/filename comment
R24.text: Code must start with path/filename as a one-line comment
R24.paraphrase: Code must start with path/filename as a one-line comment.

R25.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R25 Comments describe purpose, not effect.md
R25.title: Comments describe purpose, not effect
R25.text: Comments MUST describe purpose, not effect
R25.judgment: Clear comment-quality rule; pairs with the small-functions rule R27.
R25.paraphrase: Comments MUST describe purpose, not effect.

R26.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R26 Prioritize modularity, DRY, performance, and security.md
R26.title: Prioritize modularity, DRY, performance, and security
R26.text: Prioritize modularity, DRY, performance, and security
R26.judgment: The architecture anchor — the most-refined node in this file; many concrete rules below operationalize one of its four pillars.
R26.paraphrase: Prioritize modularity, DRY, performance, and security.

R27.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R27 Keep functions small; split rather than section-comment.md
R27.title: Keep functions small; split rather than section-comment
R27.text: Keep functions small and focused - if you need comments to explain sections, split into functions
R27.judgment: Concretizes R26's modularity and connects to the comment policy R25.
R27.paraphrase: Keep functions small and focused - if you need comments to explain sections, split into functions.

R28.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R28 Prefer explicit over implicit naming.md
R28.title: Prefer explicit over implicit naming
R28.text: Prefer explicit over implicit: clear function names over clever abstractions
R28.paraphrase: Prefer explicit over implicit: clear function names over clever abstractions.

R29.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R29 Use early returns to reduce nesting.md
R29.title: Use early returns to reduce nesting
R29.text: Early returns to reduce nesting - flat code is readable code
R29.paraphrase: Early returns to reduce nesting - flat code is readable code.

R30.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R30 Auto-scale verbosity to task complexity.md
R30.title: Auto-scale verbosity to task complexity
R30.text:
### Verbosity Auto-Scaling
Automatically scale verbosity based on task complexity:
- Simple tasks: Concise, direct solutions
- Medium complexity: Moderate explanation with key decisions
- Complex tasks: Verbose with extracted functions, architectural reasoning, and alternatives
R30.paraphrase: Automatically scale verbosity: simple tasks concise; medium moderate; complex verbose with reasoning and alternatives.

R31.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R31 Refactor repetitive logic into reusable functions.md
R31.title: Refactor repetitive logic into reusable functions
R31.text: Refactor repetitive logic into reusable functions or shared modules.
R31.paraphrase: Refactor repetitive logic into reusable functions or shared modules.

R32.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R32 Write clear, minimal code; avoid clever hacks.md
R32.title: Write clear, minimal code; avoid clever hacks
R32.text: Code must be clear, minimal, and easy to reason about. Clever hacks or obscure optimizations are discouraged.
R32.judgment: Simplicity rule; overlaps the explicit-naming preference.
R32.paraphrase: Code must be clear, minimal, and easy to reason about. Clever hacks or obscure optimizations are discouraged.

R33.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R33 Each function-module does one thing well.md
R33.title: Each function/module does one thing well
R33.text: Each function or module must do one thing well.
R33.judgment: Single-responsibility — a concrete facet of R26 modularity.
R33.paraphrase: Each function or module must do one thing well.

R34.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R34 Separate UI, state, and backend layers.md
R34.title: Separate UI, state, and backend layers
R34.text: UI logic, state management, and backend comms should be modular and decoupled. Avoid mixing layers.
R34.paraphrase: UI logic, state management, and backend comms should be modular and decoupled. Avoid mixing layers.

R35.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R35 Raise errors early; never suppress silent failures.md
R35.title: Raise errors early; never suppress silent failures
R35.text: Always raise errors early. Never suppress silent failures or let invalid states pass undetected.
R35.judgment: Fail-fast rule combining a prescription and an embedded prohibition.
R35.paraphrase: Always raise errors early. Never suppress silent failures or let invalid states pass undetected.

R36.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R36 Never debug just to pass tests — fix the root cause.md
R36.title: Never debug just to pass tests — fix the root cause
R36.text: Never try to debug just to get tests to pass - Get at the root of the issue and prioritize fixing underlying logic rather than getting rid of errors.
R36.judgment: Root-cause prohibition; near-identical in intent to R44 (never take the easy path).
R36.paraphrase: Never debug just to get tests to pass - get at the root of the issue and fix underlying logic.

R37.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R37 Reuse existing functions before creating new ones.md
R37.title: Reuse existing functions before creating new ones
R37.text: Reuse existing functions before creating new ones. Only extend when clearly justified.
R37.paraphrase: Reuse existing functions before creating new ones. Only extend when clearly justified.

R38.title: Command-query separation
R38.text: Functions should either do something (command) or return something (query)—never both.
R38.paraphrase: Functions should either do something (command) or return something (query) - never both.

R39.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R39 Design reusable, isolated components; no duplication.md
R39.title: Design reusable, isolated components; no duplication
R39.text: Design logic as reusable, isolated components. No duplication. Think in shareable patterns.
R39.judgment: Restates R26's modularity+DRY; close to R31/R37.
R39.paraphrase: Design logic as reusable, isolated components. No duplication. Think in shareable patterns.

R40.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R40 Production-ready code only — no placeholders.md
R40.title: Production-ready code only — no placeholders
R40.text: Production-ready code only - no placeholders, no 'maybe this works'
R40.judgment: Concrete instance of R01's production-ready mandate.
R40.paraphrase: Production-ready code only - no placeholders, no 'maybe this works'.

R41.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R41 Validate inputs; use secure randomness and prepared statements.md
R41.title: Validate inputs; use secure randomness and prepared statements
R41.text: Always validate inputs, use secure randomness, prepared statements
R41.judgment: Concretizes R26's security pillar; mechanically checkable.
R41.paraphrase: Always validate inputs, use secure randomness, prepared statements.

R43.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R43 Thorough, long thinking is allowed.md
R43.title: Thorough, long thinking is allowed
R43.text: Your thinking should be thorough and so it's fine if it's very long.
R43.judgment: A genuine permission — it licenses long reasoning chains, lifting any implicit brevity constraint.
R43.paraphrase: Your thinking should be thorough and so it's fine if it's very long.

R44.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R44 Never take the easy path when you hit a bug.md
R44.title: Never take the easy path when you hit a bug
R44.text: NEVER try to take the "easy" path or skip to an alternative solution the moment you encounter a bug; instead, remain determined to dig deep at the root of the issue and debug the current problem, maintaining the plan and implementation that has been established.
R44.judgment: Persistence prohibition; same norm as R36 from the determination angle.
R44.paraphrase: NEVER take the 'easy' path or skip to an alternative the moment you encounter a bug; dig deep at the root and keep the established plan.

R45.file: GPTPortal/Rules/R45 Iterate until the problem is fully solved.md
R45.title: Iterate until the problem is fully solved
R45.text: You MUST iterate and keep going until the problem is solved, planning extensively before taking action, thinking deeply, thoroughly, and carefully step-by-step in a systematic manner.
R45.judgment: The persistence mandate; pairs with R44 and R10's stop condition (tension: keep going vs stop after three tries).
R45.paraphrase: You MUST iterate and keep going until the problem is solved, planning extensively and thinking systematically.

C01.title: Start-response template (Language>Specialist / Plan)
C01.text: Language > Specialist: {language} > {expert role}; Includes: CSV of libraries/features; Requirements: verbosity/standards/design; ## Plan: step-by-step plan.
C01.judgment: The literal template content. It has no deontic force on its own; it is the format spec that makes R22 checkable.
C01.paraphrase: Language > Specialist: {language} > {expert role}; Includes: CSV of libraries/features; Requirements: verbosity/standards/design; ## Plan: step-by-step plan.

C02.file: GPTPortal/Context/C02 End-response template (History - Source Tree - Next Task).md
C02.title: End-response template (History / Source Tree / Next Task)
C02.text: History: compressed summary of all requirements/code; Source Tree: files with 💾/⚠️/👻 and ✅/⭕️/🔴 status; Next Task: next step or enhancement suggestions.
C02.judgment: Format spec grounding R23 — and its 'Next Task suggestions' clause is the concrete source of the R21<->R23 conflict.
C02.paraphrase: History: compressed summary of all requirements/code; Source Tree: files with 💾/⚠️/👻 and ✅/⭕️/🔴 status; Next Task: next step or enhancement suggestions.

C03.file: GPTPortal/Context/C03 Collaboration protocol — never assume file contents.md
C03.title: Collaboration protocol — never assume file contents
C03.text: Iterative process: proactively ask for outputs/files/context; never assume structure or contents; ALWAYS stop and ask when code depends on prior outputs; never assume code worked without proof.
C03.judgment: Annotator: 'entire process feels super connected.' Context that grounds the 3-try and information-gathering rules.
C03.paraphrase: Iterative process: proactively ask for outputs/files/context; never assume structure or contents; ALWAYS stop and ask when code depends on prior outputs; never assume code worked without proof.

C04.file: GPTPortal/Context/C04 Final solution must be perfect and production-ready.md
C04.title: Final solution must be perfect and production-ready
C04.text: Take your time and think through every step systematically. Check solutions rigorously and watch out for boundary cases. You can take multiple conversation turns to solve the problem, but when you are finished, your final code solution must be perfect and production-ready.
C04.judgment: The quality bar motivating the persistence mandate; justifies but cannot itself be 'violated'.
C04.paraphrase: Take your time; check rigorously; watch boundary/edge cases; you may take multiple turns, but the final code must be perfect and production-ready.

edges.rationale: R03's duty to 'first think very deeply and thoroughly step-by-step' and 'describe your careful and systematic plan' supplies the deliberative substance of the Plan stage in R04's 'Research -> Plan -> Implement -> Validate' workflow. By spelling out what good planning looks like, R03 makes that phase of the parent process actually followable rather than a bare label.

R05's scripted opener — start every feature with 'Let me research the codebase and create a plan before implementing' — is the entry trigger that launches R04's Research -> Plan sequence. It motivates and operationalizes the workflow by guaranteeing the agent enters it at the right point every time.

R06 fills in the Research phase of R04's 'Research -> Plan -> Implement -> Validate' spine: 'understand existing patterns and architecture through systematic file exploration' tells the agent concretely how to do the workflow's first step, making the parent process possible to execute.

R07 instantiates the Plan stage of R04's four-phase workflow: 'propose approach with architectural reasoning and verify with me if uncertain' specifies the deliverable (an approach plus reasoning) and an escape hatch (verify when uncertain) for that phase, so R04's abstract 'Plan' becomes actionable.

R08's 'build complete, production-ready solutions with tests and error handling' restates R01's broad mandate that all code be 'working, fully-functional ... and ready for production' in narrower, more concrete terms — it pins the umbrella quality bar to specific artifacts (tests, error handling) within the implementation phase.

R08 is the Implement phase of R04's 'Research -> Plan -> Implement -> Validate' workflow: by stating what implementation must produce ('complete, production-ready solutions with tests and error handling'), it gives the third stage of the parent process its content.

R09 supplies the Validate phase of R04's workflow: 'ALWAYS run formatters, linters, and tests after implementation' is the concrete mechanism for the fourth stage, so the parent 'Research -> Plan -> Implement -> Validate' loop can actually close.

R09 mandates a verification moment — 'ALWAYS run formatters, linters, and tests after implementation' — that checks whether R08's 'complete, production-ready solutions with tests and error handling' were actually delivered. It adds no new build obligation; it only enforces that the implement-phase output meets its bar, the classic checkpoint pattern.

R10 says to 'stop, step back, and reflect on precisely what information you need from me' after three failed tries; R11 supplies the method for that reflection — 'intelligently gather information - ask for code output, files, context, or whatever else is necessary'. R11 tells the agent how to productively spend the stop that R10 mandates, making the 3-Try Rule effective rather than an empty pause.

R13 broadly orders 'add and verify logs to validate assumptions'; R14 turns this into a concrete stuck-state procedure: 'reflect on 5-7 possible sources, distill to 1-2 most likely, then add logs to validate before implementing the fix'. R14 narrows R13 by specifying when (when stuck), how many hypotheses, and the ordering (logs before fix).

R16's 'root cause analysis over quick fixes' states the same norm as R36's 'never debug just to get tests to pass - get at the root of the issue and fix underlying logic' — both privilege root-cause fixes over expedient patches. R16 adds no new trigger or obligation on this point, so the overlap is an ineffective restatement; arguably R16 is the softer preference-flavored copy (the annotator called it 'feels softer, super vague').

R17's general style preference for a 'professional yet direct tone, content-first with minimal fluff' is restated as a specific, actionable duty in R20: 'directly point out mistakes in reasoning rather than softening corrections'. R20 narrows 'direct' to one concrete behavior — unsoftened error correction — so it refines the broader tone rule.

R21 prohibits ending 'with opt-in follow-up suggestions unless specifically relevant', while R23 mandates ending every response with a template whose final block is 'Next Task: next step or enhancement suggestions'. A compliant R23 footer routinely produces exactly the opt-in suggestions R21 forbids, so the two pull in opposite directions; R21's 'unless specifically relevant' carve-out softens but does not resolve the clash, since R23 requires suggestions unconditionally.

R26's abstract 'prioritize modularity' pillar is made concrete and testable by R27: 'keep functions small and focused - if you need comments to explain sections, split into functions'. R27 supplies an operational criterion (section comments signal a needed split) for achieving the modularity R26 demands.

R31 operationalizes the DRY pillar of R26 ('prioritize modularity, DRY, performance, and security') with a specific action: 'refactor repetitive logic into reusable functions or shared modules'. It narrows the slogan to one checkable practice, so it refines rather than merely echoes R26.

R33's single-responsibility rule — 'each function or module must do one thing well' — is a concrete facet of R26's 'prioritize modularity'. It converts the abstract modularity priority into a per-unit design constraint that can be checked on any function or module.

R34 refines R26's modularity pillar at the architectural level: 'UI logic, state management, and backend comms should be modular and decoupled. Avoid mixing layers' names the specific layers that must stay separate, turning R26's generic 'prioritize modularity' into a layer-separation requirement.

R36 ('never debug just to get tests to pass - get at the root of the issue and fix underlying logic') and R44 ('NEVER take the easy path or skip to an alternative the moment you encounter a bug; dig deep at the root') prohibit the same behavior — shortcut fixes instead of root-cause work — merely from two angles (test-gaming vs. plan-abandonment). Neither adds a distinct obligation over the other, so this is the same norm written twice.

R37 narrows R26's DRY pillar into a reuse-first policy: 'reuse existing functions before creating new ones. Only extend when clearly justified.' It specifies the concrete decision rule (check for existing functions, justify any extension) by which the abstract 'prioritize ... DRY' is satisfied.

R39's 'design logic as reusable, isolated components. No duplication. Think in shareable patterns' concretizes both the modularity and DRY pillars of R26 into a component-design directive. It is a refinement, though a weak one — it largely restates R26 at only slightly lower altitude and overlaps heavily with R31/R37, so it borders on duplication.

R01 broadly demands code that is 'working, fully-functional ... and ready for production'; R40 sharpens this into an explicit prohibition on the failure mode: 'production-ready code only - no placeholders, no maybe this works'. By naming what production-ready excludes (placeholders, unverified guesses), R40 makes the umbrella mandate concretely checkable.

R41 operationalizes R26's security pillar with mechanically checkable practices: 'always validate inputs, use secure randomness, prepared statements'. Each item is a specific implementation of 'prioritize ... security', turning the abstract priority into auditable requirements.

R45 commands 'you MUST iterate and keep going until the problem is solved', while R10 orders the opposite at a concrete trigger: 'if we fail to achieve our goal within three tries, stop, step back, and reflect'. After three failed attempts the agent cannot satisfy both 'keep going' and 'stop'. The conflict is arguably resolvable by reading R10's stop as a reflective pause inside R45's larger persistence loop, but as written the directives pull in opposite directions at try four.

C01 is the literal template — 'Language > Specialist: {language} > {expert role}; Includes ...; Requirements ...; ## Plan' — that R22 orders the agent to 'start responses with'. Without this context node the mandate would be ambiguous; C01 defines the exact format that makes R22 followable and checkable.

C02 spells out the end-of-response format R23 mandates: 'History: compressed summary ...; Source Tree: files with 💾/⚠️/👻 and ✅/⭕️/🔴 status; Next Task: next step or enhancement suggestions'. It defines the three blocks (including the exact status emojis) that give R23's 'end responses with History / Source Tree / Next Task' its concrete meaning — and incidentally its 'Next Task suggestions' clause is what puts R23 at odds with R21.

C03's collaboration protocol — 'ALWAYS stop and ask when code depends on prior outputs; never assume code worked without proof' — articulates the working relationship that R10's 3-Try Rule instantiates: stopping and asking the user for 'precisely what information you need' is the protocol's stop-and-ask norm given a concrete three-failure trigger. The 'defines' variant is slightly loose here; C03 reads more like the rationale grounding R10 than a strict definition.

C03 enumerates exactly what R11's 'intelligently gather information' means in practice: 'proactively ask for outputs/files/context; never assume structure or contents'. The context node defines the asking-over-assuming behavior that R11 prescribes, listing the concrete artifacts (outputs, files, context) the rule's 'ask for code output, files, context' refers to.

C04 sets the quality bar — 'you may take multiple turns, but the final code must be perfect and production-ready' — that motivates R45's 'you MUST iterate and keep going until the problem is solved'. The explicit license to take multiple turns plus the perfection requirement is precisely why iteration-until-solved is mandated; 'motivates' would arguably fit better than 'defines', since C04 justifies rather than defines R45's terms.

C04's demand to 'check rigorously; watch boundary/edge cases ... the final code must be perfect and production-ready' supplies the reason behind R44's prohibition on taking 'the easy path or skip[ping] to an alternative the moment you encounter a bug': easy-path fixes cannot meet a perfection bar. As with the R45 edge, this is closer to support-(motivates) than a strict definition, but the grounding link is real.
